description: An occupational health technician needs to document a worker's hexavalent chromium exposure. The patient, Roberto Carlos, presented to the occupational health clinic with chronic epistaxis and painful skin sores ('chrome holes'). Complete the occupational toxicology documentation in GNU Health: 1) Add an active diagnosis of 'Toxic effect of chromium and its compounds' (ICD-10 T56.2) for Roberto. 2) Create a clinical evaluation documenting his vital signs exactly as: Temperature 37.0°C and Heart Rate 75 bpm. 3) Prescribe targeted therapy for the ulcers: Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C), Mupirocin, or Bacitracin. 4) Order at least 2 baseline laboratory tests for biological monitoring (e.g., Urinalysis, CBC, CMP, or Heavy Metal screen). 5) Schedule an ENT or Dermatology follow-up appointment for Roberto between 10 and 20 days from today. Login credentials: username 'admin', password 'gnusolidario'.
